image:image.image:title: Generation vs. demand map — U.S. power plants over data-center load
National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid
image:image.image:caption: Major U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid. Source: public EIA / CBRE / JLL (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: Map of Northern Virginia data-center clusters over the power grid
image:image.image:caption: Northern Virginia data-center clusters over the power grid. Source: public EIA / CBRE / JLL (late 2025); cluster locations approximate.

image:image.image:title: Map of Texas data-center clusters and power plants over the ERCOT grid
image:image.image:caption: Texas data-center clusters and generation over the ERCOT grid. Source: public EIA / ERCOT (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: Queued versus operational capacity in ERCOT
image:image.image:caption: Queued ≠ built: how little of the ERCOT large-load queue is operational. Source: ERCOT / Latitude Media (Nov 2025).

image:image.image:title: Map of Texas data-center clusters and power plants over the ERCOT grid, including Dallas–Fort Worth
image:image.image:caption: Texas clusters and generation over the ERCOT grid; DFW is the northern anchor. Source: public EIA / ERCOT (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Silicon Valley is the constrained West Coast node
image:image.image:caption: National context — Silicon Valley is the mature, supply-capped West Coast node. Source: public EIA / CBRE (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Chicago anchors the Midwest
image:image.image:caption: National context — Chicago is the Midwest's dominant data-center node. Source: public EIA / CBRE (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; the New York tri-state anchors the Northeast
image:image.image:caption: National context — the tri-state is the Northeast's connectivity and financial-latency anchor. Source: public EIA / CBRE (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Iowa is the wind-powered heartland node
image:image.image:caption: National context — Iowa is the wind-powered heartland node, drawing hyperscale AI training onto a low-cost, low-carbon grid. Source: public EIA / reporting (end-2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Memphis is the emergent Mid-South node in TVA territory
image:image.image:caption: National context — Memphis is an emergent node in TVA territory, defined less by market size than by how it got powered. Source: public EIA / reporting (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Hillsboro is the Pacific Northwest node
image:image.image:caption: National context — Hillsboro is the Pacific Northwest node, anchored to cheap hydro and the transpacific fiber landings. Source: public EIA / CBRE (end-2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: Map of Georgia data-center clusters and power plants over the grid
image:image.image:caption: Georgia data-center clusters and generation over the grid. Source: public EIA / CBRE (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: Map of Arizona data-center clusters and power plants over the grid
image:image.image:caption: Arizona data-center clusters and generation over the grid. Source: public EIA / CBRE (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: Map of Ohio data-center clusters and power plants over the grid
image:image.image:caption: Ohio data-center clusters and generation over the grid. Source: public EIA / CBRE (late 2025); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Salt Lake City and Utah's Wasatch Front is the Mountain West node
image:image.image:caption: National context — Utah's Wasatch Front is the Mountain West node, drawing on cheap power and a legislated right to self-supply. Source: public EIA / CBRE (2026); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Reno and Northern Nevada is the Great Basin node
image:image.image:caption: National context — Northern Nevada is the Great Basin node, built on fast permitting and cheap land. Source: public EIA / CBRE (2026); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Nashville and Middle Tennessee sit in TVA territory in the Southeast
image:image.image:caption: National context — Middle Tennessee is a TVA-territory node in the fast-growing Southeast. Source: public EIA / CBRE (2026); clusters approximate.

image:image.image:title: National map of U.S. data-center clusters over the power grid; Kansas City is the emerging Midwest node straddling Missouri and Kansas
image:image.image:caption: National context — Kansas City is an emerging Midwest node in SPP territory, straddling Missouri and Kansas. Source: public EIA / CBRE (2026); clusters approximate.
